topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The knock on the door startled me. I hadn't expected anything. My jaw tightened as I peered through the peephole. A delivery person stood there, holding a plain brown box addressed to 'J. Smith.' My stomach clenched. It was the former tenant’s mail, a physical manifestation of the unwelcome intrusion. I dragged myself to the door, my movements stiff. When I took the package, the cardboard felt heavy, imbued with the ghost of someone else's life. I didn't bother with the return address; I just shoved it into the darkest corner of the hallway closet, a silent acknowledgment of the inconvenience.

[story 2]

I found the package tucked behind the recycling bin, a stark, unwelcome item amidst the usual refuse. My hands trembled slightly as I lifted it. It was clearly meant for the unit above, a tangible reminder of the careless way things had ended. A slow, hot flush crept up my neck. I stared at the shipping label, the handwriting unfamiliar yet instantly recognizable as belonging to someone I actively wished to forget. I didn't want it. I wanted silence, and this delivery was just another unwelcome noise in my quiet existence. I crumpled the paper once, twice, before smoothing it out and placing it on the kitchen counter, a deliberate act of setting it aside, though the tension in my shoulders remained stubbornly tight.

[story 3]

The doorbell chimed, and I braced myself for another solicitor or a misplaced delivery. Instead, it was a small parcel. When I opened it, the contents—a box of old photographs—made me pause. My breath hitched. They were pictures of their messy living room, moments I had spent weeks trying to scrub from my memory. A profound sense of resentment settled over me, heavy and cold in my chest. I didn't look at the photos for long. I simply set the box down with a dull thud, the sound echoing too loudly in the empty apartment. I walked away without a word, the weight of the unwanted possessions clinging to my fingertips like dust.
